australia.title: Man accused of attempting to storm Trump dinner to remain in jail
summary: Summarize this news article:

A man accused of trying to storm the White House Correspondents' Association dinner with guns and knives and attempting to kill US President Donald Trump has agreed to remain jailed for now while he awaits trial.

Cole Tomas Allen did not enter a plea during his brief appearance in federal court days after authorities say he ran through a magnetometer at the Washington Hilton while holding a long gun and disrupted one of the highest-profile annual events in the nation's capital.
Mr Allen was injured during Saturday night's attack but was not shot.
A Secret Service officer was shot but was wearing a bullet-resistant vest and survived, officials say.

Prosecutors have said they believe Mr Allen fired his shotgun at least once and that a Secret Service agent fired five shots. They have not publicly confirmed that it was Allen's bullet that struck the agent's vest.
Court documents include these images of a selfie that Cole Tomas Allen took in his hotel room, according to prosecutors.(Supplie Summarize this news article:
d: US District Court for the District of Columbia)

In court papers pressing for Mr Allen's continued detention, prosecutors wrote Wednesday that Mr Allen took a picture of himself in his hotel room just minutes before the incident, and that he was outfitted with an ammunition bag, a shoulder gun holster and a sheathed knife.

In a message that authorities say sheds light on his motive, Mr Allen referred to himself as a "Friendly Federal Assassin" and alluded obliquely to grievances over a range of Trump administration actions.

Mr Allen's lawyers agreed during the brief hearing before US Magistrate Moxila Upadhyaya to keep their client behind bars for now after initially arguing in court papers that Allen should be released.

In a court filing Wednesday, the defence wrote that the government's case is "based upon inferences drawn about Mr Allen's intent that raise more questions than answers" and noted that Mr Allen's writings never mentioned Mr Trump by name.
Court documents show Cole To

australia.title: The Liberals should easily win Nepean by-election, but politics is changing
summary: Summarize this news article:
In seven months' time, a 12-year-old state Labor government faces voters in Victoria.
Labor's support has slid significantly over the past few years and the party is campaigning for a fourth term in office, loaded up with the baggage that long-term governments tend to accumulate.
In a "normal" political world, the Liberal Party would be kicking with the breeze and working to form government.

This weekend's by-election in the affluent Mornington Peninsula seat of Nepean, which it has held for all but one term in the past 40 years, would be a leisurely warm-up in the lead-up to the general election.
With no Labor candidate contesting, it should be a cakewalk for the Liberals.
But we are no longer in a normal political world.
The Victorian Liberal Party has been at war with itself, compounded by a federal Coalition going through an existential crisis of its own.
The circumstances offormer Victorian Deputy Liberal leader and Nepean MP Sam Groth's sudden exitand local unrest about the subse Summarize this news article:
quent preselection of Mornington Peninsula Mayor Anthony Marsh, who joined the party just weeks beforehand, also don't help.

Nepean covers the western tip of the peninsula and takes in wealthy bayside communities such as Sorrento and Portsea, where the Liberals won more than two-thirds of the two-party preferred vote in 2022.
The seat also takes in Dromana, Rosebud and McCrae, where incomes are a bit lower and Labor was more competitive in 2022.

The Liberals are still the favourites to win this weekend's by-election, but Nepean's demographics offer some opportunities for challengers to make inroads and peel off support from unhappy Liberal voters.
The first challenger is One Nation candidate Darren Hercus, a civil engineer who runs a joist and truss manufacturing business in Dromana.

One Nation proved in South Australia's March state election that it is attracting significant support in new parts of the country. There are no neat comparisons with seats in South Australia, but we know t

australia.title: How two court cases could change supermarket pricing rules
summary: Summarize this news article:
You can imagine the alarm bells going off for Paul Harker as he browsed the colourful aisles of his local Woolworths.
The senior Woolworths' executive was searching for the bright red Prices Dropped tickets that promise long-term savings on household staples.
But Harker wasn't looking for a bargain вҖ” he was trying to work out if the supermarket giant had a serious problem.
Mid-way through 2022, the Australian Competition and Consumer Commission (ACCC) had come knocking, probing its prominent discounting program.
That would eventually result in the bombshell lawsuit Woolies now faces in which it is accused of misleading shoppers with fake discounts on more than 260 items вҖ” something it denies.
It was in the laundry aisle that Harker spotted his first problem.
The Prices Dropped ticket for a Fab Laundry powder offered a huge saving вҖ” down from a higher "was" price of $14 to just $8.
Harker immediately realised the $14 "was" price tag was "too high" compared to the discounted price.
He ya Summarize this news article:
nked the Fab powder from the Prices Dropped program, later finding out the $14 price tag was the supplier's proposed recommended retail price.
The federal court case against Woolies is expected to wrap up this week and another remarkably similar one against Coles is awaiting judgment.
The outcome of both cases will ultimately reveal whether supermarket shoppers have been screwed over with dubious discounts.
Both cases against the major supermarkets have the potential to reshape retail pricing.(ABC News: Billy Cooper)
During the proceedings, the focus has been on trying to establish whether grocery items were given genuine reductions.
This meant items needed to have a higher previous "was" price for long enough to ensure it was considered legitimate.
But the problem is there has been no clear red line that states how long that period should be and experts say picking one is highly problematic.
The pricing patterns of these six products show how Coles stands accused of using its Down Dow

india.title: As Trump nukes science, is India's brain drain ready for 'brain regain'?
summary: Summarize this news article:

He thinks diet soda kills cancer cells inside the body when consumed because it scorches grass when poured on it. He claims noise from windmills causes cancer. He most famously mused about injecting disinfectants into the body to kill the covid virus.

Even in a presidency not known for scientific temper, US President Donald TrumpГўВҖВҷs latest move has landed with unusual force: the wholesale dismissal of the National Science Board, the independent 22-member body, including three Indian-Americans, that oversees the National Science Foundation (NSF), AmericaГўВҖВҷs primary engine for funding basic research.

Among those removed were three prominent scientists of Indian origin: Sudarsanam Babu, a leading figure in advanced manufacturing at Oak Ridge National Laboratory; Arun Majumdar, dean of StanfordГўВҖВҷs Doerr School of Sustainability and a former US energy official; and Suresh Garimella, a noted thermal sciences expert and university president. Their presence on the board had reflected the gl Summarize this news article:
obal character of American science, and their removal comes at a time when there are calls from some prominent Indian tech elites urging their return.

Created by Congress in 1950 and designed to outlast political cycles, the NSB was meant to insulate science policy from partisan swings. Its abrupt termination ГўВҖВ” delivered via a terse email last week ГўВҖВ” has instead become a symbol of a broader dismantling of the countryГўВҖВҷs scientific architecture.

Over the past year, the Trump administration has fired advisory panels, canceled more than 1,400 NSF grants, and proposed sweeping budget cuts to major research agencies ГўВҖВ” including reductions of over 50 percent to NSF funding and deep cuts to the National Institutes of Health (NIH), NASAГўВҖВҷs science programs, and the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC). Fourteen NSF advisory committees have been eliminated.
The consequences are already rippling through laboratories and universities. Funding uncertainty has forced hiring free

india.title: 'Murder of democracy': TMC claims poll fraud in Kolkata strongroom; EC counters, BJP reacts
summary: Summarize this news article:

NEW DELHI: The Trinamool Congress (TMC) alleged an ГўВҖВңelectoral fraudГўВҖВқ by the Bharatiya Janata Party (BJP) and the Election Commission, claiming tampering of ballots ahead of the May 4 counting for the West Bengal assembly elections.

The party shared CCTV footage purportedly showing the interior of a room with ballots, the authenticity of which has not been independently verified by The Times of India. The EC, however, responding to the video footage, issued a clarification listing out the measures being taken to ensure fair vote counting.
BJP also responded to the allegation and said that TMC is spreading rumours and is deliberately creating a false narrative because they anticipated a poor performance in the assembly elections.
TMC Stages Protest Outside EVM Strong Room In Kolkata, Shares CCTV Footage Alleging Irregularities
According to the TMC, the video shows individuals opening ballot units in the absence of authorised officials, an act it claims violates established electoral  Summarize this news article:
protocols and undermines the democratic process.

TMC in the post also shared about the protest being led by its leaders, Shashi Panja and Kunal Ghosh, at Netaji Indoor Stadium, further stating that the Bengal CM Mamata Banerjee is expected to arrive too.
Sharing the video on X, they wrote, "Alarming! This is the murder of democracy in broad daylight.

CCTV footage has exposed how BJP, in active collusion with the EC, is opening ballot boxes without the presence of any relevant party stakeholders. This is gross electoral fraud being committed openly with the full knowledge and protection of the Election Commission."

It further alleged, "They have tried every dirty trick, name deletion, voter intimidation, Central Force terror, cash flooding, and failed miserably. Now, in sheer desperation, they have stooped to tampering with EVMs. But Bengal is not Maharashtra, Delhi, or Bihar. We will not sit silently and watch them loot our democracy."
The official handle of the Trinamool Congress furt

india.title: вҖҳToday was my dayвҖҷ: Shubman Gill reveals вҖҳlucky breakвҖҷ in GT win over RCB
summary: Summarize this news article:
summed it up best after Gujarat TitansГўВҖВҷ clinical chase ГўВҖВ” sometimes, momentum just finds you. And on Thursday night in Ahmedabad, it certainly did.

Leading from the front, Gill played a decisive hand in Gujarat TitansГўВҖВҷ four-wicket win over Royal Challengers Bengaluru, later admitting that an early slice of luck set the tone for his match-defining knock.

ГўВҖВңFeels very satisfying. On a wicket like that, to restrict a team like them to 155 is very nice,ГўВҖВқ Gill, who scored 43 off just 18 balls, said after the win. ГўВҖВңFirst ball I got a bit lucky and then I thought today was my day. I was in the zone and confident of taking the bowling on.ГўВҖВқ
That ГўВҖВңluckyГўВҖВқ moment came early against
when an inside edge went for a four, but Gill made it count in style. He quickly shifted gears, putting RCB under pressure with an aggressive approach in the powerplay.
ГўВҖВңThe energy was quite high after the second over. I was just feeling I was in the zone,ГўВҖВқ he added, reflecting on his mindset during  Summarize this news article:
the chase.
GillГўВҖВҷs attacking intent, combined with brisk contributions from
and late composure from Rahul Tewatia, ensured GT stayed ahead despite a brief middle-order wobble.
ГўВҖВңTewatia is a very important player for us. That was one of the conversations we had in the timeout. He got some nice runs and everyone got a hit in the middle,ГўВҖВқ Gill noted.
On the other side, RCB skipper Rajat Patidar acknowledged that GillГўВҖВҷs powerplay assault proved decisive.

ГўВҖВңObviously, the score was not good enough on this track,ГўВҖВқ Patidar admitted. ГўВҖВңBut the way Shubman played, especially in the powerplay, he put us under pressure and took the game away.ГўВҖВқ
Despite posting just 155, RCB managed to stretch the contest deeper than expected, something Patidar viewed as a positive.
ГўВҖВңI think the way we bowled and stretched it to 15-16 overs is a positive sign. There was good grass cover that helped the fast bowlers,ГўВҖВқ he said.

india.title: 'Preparing ground for their defeat': BJP takes jab at TMC over vote rigging claims
summary: Summarize this news article:
on Thursday hit out at the All India Trinamool Comgress (TMC), dismissing the latter allegations on postal ballot box tampering.
On the accusations that the BJP ГўВҖВңin active collusionГўВҖВқ with the

ГўВҖВңis opening ballot boxes without the presence of any relevant party stakeholdersГўВҖВқ, BJP leader Tapas Roy said that the ruling party leaders ГўВҖВңare spreading rumoursГўВҖВқ as they ГўВҖВңare preparing ground for their defeatГўВҖВқ in the assembly polls.

Roy arrived at Netaji Indoor Stadium as TMC leaders Kunal Ghosh and Shashi Panja staged a protest over the allegations. ГўВҖВңWe will deploy two persons to look after the strong room...They (TMC) are just spreading rumours as they are preparing ground for their defeat...Three-layer security arrangements are here,ГўВҖВқ he told reporters, as quoted by ANI.

"They cannot do this. They cannot assemble in front of the strong room. No sit-in demonstration or slogan shouting should happen in front of a strong room. This is wrong. Why did the Kolkata Police allow it Summarize this news article:
?...What manipulation are they (TMC) accusing us of? Two of us had come here when we heard about it. We came when we were told to visit here," he added.
This came as TMC leader Shashi Panja accused BJP leaders of creating a ruckus, saying the protest had been peaceful before their arrival. ГўВҖВңWe were sitting on the dharna peacefully...BJP
leaders created a ruckus here,ГўВҖВқ she said.

TMC leader Kunal Ghosh also levelled allegations against the BJP, questioning why he and others were not allowed entry while Roy was. ГўВҖВңWe were removed, but Tapas da went inside. What do you call this? That is like their own party office. These agent central police with the BJP people... it is not in the hands of the Kolkata police; even the Kolkata police personnel are now all appointed by the Election Commission. As a result, our... Hey, if he is a candidate, we are also candidates. Will you remove a candidate?ГўВҖВқ he said.
